title: Weather Report: Monsoon fury batters several states, over 10 dead in rain-related incidents
content:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced on Thursday that the southwest monsoon has fully advanced into the remaining parts of Rajasthan, Haryana, and Punjab, thereby covering the entire country.
• India recorded a large surplus of rainfall in July, receiving 101.9 mm against a normal of 73.8 mm for the first nine days of the month.
• In Surat, rain-related incidents claimed the lives of 17 people over the past few days, leading Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel to visit the city for review.

title: Israel willing to join future US strikes on Iran if requested, says report
content:
• The initial military campaign by the US, which Israel joined, was launched on February 28, targeting Iran's leadership and strategic military infrastructure.
• US strikes hit roughly 90 Iranian targets, an increase from around 80 targets hit earlier in the week, including missile and drone launch sites and the perimeter of Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant.
• US strikes reportedly killed 17 people and specifically targeted the Tehran–Mashhad railway line, disrupting routes to Mashhad where Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's burial was taking place.

title: India tops intangible asset investment growth, says global report
content:
• India emerged as the world's fastest-growing major economy in investment in knowledge-based assets in 2022-23, with spending rising 7.9%, according to a World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) report.
• India's intangible investment reached $78 billion in 2023, exceeding that of several European economies.
• Software and databases accounted for nearly 45% of India's total intangible investment in 2023, marking the highest share among all economies covered in the WIPO report.

title: RBI faces $100 billion challenge after record currency defense
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) short dollar forward position ballooned to a record $106.7 billion in May.
• To attract capital, the RBI has offered to fully cover hedging costs for banks raising three- to five-year foreign-currency deposits from non-resident Indians.
• As of May, nearly $29 billion of the RBI’s net short forward position is maturing within three months, and about $51 billion over a one-year horizon.

title: India raises questions at WTO over interim arrangement to implement e-comm pact
content:
• India has raised questions at the WTO regarding the interim arrangements adopted by 66 countries to implement the Agreement on Electronic Commerce (ECA), which lacks the consensus required for incorporation into the WTO framework.
• The Agreement on Electronic Commerce (ECA) was negotiated under the Joint Statement Initiative, launched on the sidelines of the WTO’s 11th Ministerial Conference in 2017.
• India has sought clarification on the legal authority under which the WTO Director-General is acting as the depositary for the ECA, as the Marrakesh Agreement provides for this role only for WTO agreements and Annex 4 plurilateral agreements.

title: India raises questions at WTO over interim arrangement to implement e-commerce pact
content:
• India has raised questions at the WTO over an interim arrangement for an e-commerce pact agreed upon by only 66 member countries.
• The WTO's General Council was requested on February 18 and December 16, 2025, to add the e-commerce pact to Annex 4 of WTO agreements, but no consensus was reached.
• On March 28 this year, 66 countries issued a declaration on interim arrangements as a pathway to enter into force the Agreement on Electronic Commerce (ECA).

title: RBI launches 3 key surveys to get input for monetary policy
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) launched three key surveys: 'Inflation Expectations Survey of Households', 'Rural Consumer Confidence Survey', and 'Urban Consumer Confidence Survey', to gather critical inputs for formulating monetary policy.
• The July 2026 round of the 'Inflation Expectations Survey of Households' and the 'Urban Consumer Confidence Survey' are being conducted across 19 cities, while the 'Rural Consumer Confidence Survey' (July 2026 round) covers households across 31 states/UTs.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, which will utilize the inputs from these surveys, is scheduled for August 3 to 5, 2026.

title: Biz positive on GST after 9 years; simpler compliance, lesser disputes key focus areas ahead: Survey
content:
• GST was rolled out on July 1, 2017.
• GST subsumed 17 indirect taxes and 13 cesses upon its introduction.
• Inclusion of oil and gas within the GST framework is identified as a key future reform priority by the FICCI GST Survey.

title: 'Exclusively peaceful purposes': Albanese confirms Australian uranium supply deal with India
content:
• Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese confirmed a deal on Thursday with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi for Australia to supply uranium to India for its nuclear energy industry.
• India has a target of achieving 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047, driving its interest in Australian uranium reserves.
• Beyond the uranium supply, Australia and India also agreed to deepen cooperation in renewables, critical minerals, and green hydrogen.

title: Global economy resilient despite West Asia war, but risks persist: IEA, IMF, World Bank, WTO
content:
• The assessment on global economic resilience was made by the heads of the International Energy Agency (IEA), International Monetary Fund (IMF), World Bank Group (WBG), and World Trade Organization (WTO) during a high-level coordination group meeting on July 7.
• Fuel and fertiliser prices have eased since their previous meeting in June, helping to mitigate some inflationary pressures, although uncertainty and the war's economic impact could linger.
• The institutions urged vigilance to uphold the principle of freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, a key trade route facing disruptions, and globally.
